items.title: Family 'devastated' after alleged indecent assault of disabled child at school

children.content: A Northern Territory family has accused the Department of Education of failing in its duty of care for their 12-year-old girl, after she was allegedly indecently assaulted at a school that caters for children with disabilities.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a child sitting on a bed with an adult sitting alongside them.

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a child sitting on a bed with an adult sitting alongside them.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: Matt Wright representing himself in lawsuit brought by co-star's widow

children.content: A Federal Court case against celebrity crocodile wrangler Matt Wright could take another year before making it to trial. The lawsuit has been brought by Danielle Wilson, the widow of Mr Wright's TV co-star Chris "Willow" Wilson, who died in a 2022 chopper crash.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a man with blue eyes, wearing a light-coloured shirt as he walks out of court.

items.title: NT government opens door to private development in four national parks

children.content: The Northern Territory government says opening up national parks to private investors will unlock their "tourism potential", but an expert fears the move could mean less access for locals and budget-conscious visitors.

image.altText: Wangi Falls in Litchfield National Park

items.title: Outback hubs prepare for 'amazingly majestic' 2028 solar eclipse

children.content: About 10,000 eclipse chasers are expected to descend on the Northern Territory's Barkly region in two years to stand in the shadow of the moon.

gallery.altText: A man wearing special glasses over his eyes.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing special glasses over his eyes.

items.title: Captain Cook Shopping Centre sold and signs removed in town's new chapter

children.content: British explorer James Cook never visited Nhulunbuy on his voyages, but his namesake shopping complex stood there for decades — until now.

image.altText: Two men remove a sign from a fence.

items.title: Rural suburb flagged as home for proposed $40b 'hyperscale' data centre

children.content: A proposed massive new data centre would require more than six times the total demand of the Darwin and Katherine electricity grids to operate and be powered by gas piped direct from the Northern Territory's Beetaloo Basin.

image.altText: An AI generated image of the outside of the proposed facility.

items.title: Friends turned millionaires take tech company shares battle to court

children.content: The millionaire co-founder of an Australian video tech company behind some of Hollywood’s biggest blockbusters has told the Federal Court a decade of hostility by his fellow shareholders has unfairly barred him from selling his lucrative stake in the billion-dollar business.

image.altText: A group of men ride horses in a ranch.

items.title: Storm shrug off sluggish start to punish Souths

children.content: After an awful start against South Sydney the Storm have rebounded well to take the lead before halftime. Follow live.

image.altText: A man runs the ball during a rugby league match

items.title: Reginald Chard, Australia's last surviving Kokoda veteran, dies at 102

children.content: Australia's last surviving Kokoda campaign veteran, Reginald James Chard OAM, has died aged 102.

items.title: Cats surge against Dees, Eagles up for derby fight against Dockers

children.content: Melbourne hosts Geelong at the MCG with one club trying to stay in the top six and the other trying to break in, before the Dockers and Eagles meet in the Western Derby. Follow live.

items.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding

children.content: Police say they stopped the 22-year-old in a BMW Coupe about 1am today and allege he was travelling 109 kilometres per hour on the Anzac Bridge.

image.altText: A man rips at his jersey while celebrating on the soccer field.

items.title: Islanders sang hymns and kept vigil over body of teenager washed ashore

children.content: Twenty-seven people including families with young children went missing after two boats capsized in rough seas in Papua New Guinea's north-east on Saturday.

items.title: Rare eye disorder linked to Ozempic-like medications

children.content: The Therapeutic Goods Administration has issued a safety warning for a rare, but serious risk of vision impairment from taking GLP-1-based drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy.

image.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

items.title: Bondi attack had traumatic effect on MP's son, royal commission hears

children.content: Liberal MP Julian Leeser tells the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion that his young son became too scared to attend Jewish events after last year's Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Julian Leeser Liberal MP for Berowra stands outdoors and looks at the camera

items.title: Have the AFL's rule changes worked? Not exactly

children.content: A raft of AFL rule changes for the 2026 season have either excited or horrified a range of fans — Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson examine the changes and whether they have had the desired impact.

image.altText: A Fremantle AFL ruckman leaps high to tap the ball as an Essendon opponent stands watching.

items.title: How and how often should you be cleaning out your freezer?

children.content: When it comes to kitchen cleaning, the freezer is the last frontier — a forgotten wasteland where food goes to die. But it's not hard to clean, and there are good reasons to do it.

image.altText: A woman wearing an apron reaches a hand into an open freezer, which is part of an upright fridge/freezer combo.

items.title: Adolescence creator tackles teen rehab very differently in The Good Boy

children.content: Two-time ARIA Award-winning music artist Budjerah has just released his long-awaited debut album and announced an official tour.

items.title: Warning spy laws too broad and could undermine democracy

children.content: A major review warns parts of Australia's national security laws could undermine democracy and press freedom.

items.title: Automakers in shock as China takes over

children.content: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

items.title: Drivers urged not to panic-buy as fuel price hikes nowhere near record high

children.content: Australians are being urged by a peak motorist association not to panic-buy fuel amid a flare-up of tensions in the Middle East, saying prices are nowhere near April's record high.

items.title: AI has changed what criminals can do with your stolen Origin data

children.content: The theft of names, addresses and phone numbers may seem less serious than stolen bank details, but cyber experts say AI is changing what criminals can do with that information.

items.title: Aboriginal man died after being handcuffed in Adelaide CBD, police say

children.content: South Australian Police say they will treat an internal investigation into the death of a 23-year-old Aboriginal man in custody with the "utmost importance".

items.title: Child abuse reporting scheme sees hundreds of complaints in first weeks

children.content: The reportable scheme launched July 1, brought forward by 12 months, providing a consistent framework for reporting sexual offences, violence, and neglect.

image.altText: Children's toys, shoes, coloring pens.

items.title: Worker dead, another injured as truck rolls over vehicle at Queensland mine

children.content: One man is dead and another has been seriously injured after their vehicle was struck by a giant mining truck at a Central Queensland coal site.

image.altText: Bunches of flowers have sit on a moniment to remember deceased mine workers.

children.content: The RSPCA is investigating reports of kangaroos being deliberately run over in Albany, Western Australia.

items.title: Conspiracy theories about AusAlert test spreading online

children.content: From wrapping your phone in foil to secret phones, ABC NEWS Verify takes a look into some of these false claims and real concerns about AusAlert to try to answer a few questions.

items.title: Man linked to Isla Bell death fails to have case thrown out

children.content: Marat Ganiev was seeking a permanent stay on his case, with his lawyer arguing the multiple changes to the prosecution case were an abuse of process.

items.title: Brown slams Crows coach for his response to Quaynor booing

children.content: The first former AFL player to come out as bisexual, Mitch Brown, has criticised Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks for not openly condemning the booing of Collingwood's Isaac Quaynor by Crows fans.

image.altText: A Collingwood player looks up as he holds the ball in one hand during a game at Adelaide Oval.

items.title: Sam Kerr says A-League must find way to bring Matildas back, pay more

children.content: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is already thriving in the next chapter of her career with Gotham FC in the NWSL, but she wants the A-League Women to get back to its previous glory days, with more investment in the semi-professional league.

items.title: Swimmer praises Rinehart for bonuses amid warning to not thank billionaire

children.content: Swimming star Kyle Chalmers praises billionaire Gina Rinehart over bonuses for Commonwealth Games medals, as a report suggests athletes have been told not to thank the mining magnate.

image.altText: Australian swimmer Kyle Chalmers speaks to the media at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

items.title: Edmund Rice Education to pay out victim-survivors of Christian Brothers

children.content: Victim-survivors of abuse by members of the Christian Brothers organisation will be able to receive compensation for civil claims under a new payout proposal.

image.altText: A necklace with a wooden church cross and Jesus on the cross sits on a table.

items.title: After Brat, Charli xcx asks a bigger question about legacy

children.content: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.
